title: Resolve node-to-item links by qualified id across kinds
description:
Make node-to-item link labels resolve to their addressed item by qualified id, across all four kinds. A label in each shipped namespace (`satisfies:`, `per:`, `mitigates:`/`resolves:`, `enforces:`) on a node resolves to the item it addresses, whether that item is a `requirement`, `decision`, `risk`, or `invariant`. An unresolvable qualified id is reported as a finding rather than silently dropped, so dangling links surface during validation.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: For each shipped namespace (`satisfies:`, `per:`, `mitigates:`/`resolves:`, `enforces:`), a label of the form `<ns>:<scope>/<self-id>` resolves to the addressed item of the corresponding kind.
- [hard] REQ-02: Link resolution works across all four kinds: `requirement`, `decision`, `risk`, and `invariant`.
- [hard] REQ-03: An unresolvable qualified id is reported as a finding, not silently dropped.
- [hard] REQ-04: The behavior is covered by `cargo test`.
